name: Frost
description:
'''Arturo R. Molina Jr.''' (born May 31, 1964), better known as '''Frost''' (originally '''Kid Frost'''), is an American rapper, songwriter and record producer. He charted in the 1990s with his first four albums: ''[[Hispanic Causing Panic]]'', ''[[East Side Story (Kid Frost album)|East Side Story]]'', ''[[Smile Now, Die Later]]'' and ''[[When Hell.A. Freezes Over]]''. His most successful single is "[[La Raza (song)|La Raza]]" which hit number 6 on the rap songs chart in August 1990. His 1990 debut album is credited as the first [[Latin hip-hop]] album.

Frost's music entered the [[Billboard 200]] again in 2002 with the album ''[[Still Up in This Shit!]]''. He is the father of record producer [[Scoop DeVille]].
